behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE push this to System: rename detection is produced by real `git` behavior (e.g., similarity thresholds and diff flags), and a mocked git response could hide a real failure where the CLI invokes git incorrectly and receives add/delete instead of rename. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is only about git change categorization, with no AI/ML decision or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert the report increments `renamed` only (not `added`+`deleted`) for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: binary diff handling depends on real git subprocess output and encoding/format edge cases. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing diff text can miss crashes triggered only by real binary diff output paths. REJECTION-GATE: no AI/ML output quality or multi-step agent loop is described.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., image), run the CLI subprocess, and assert exit code is success and binary changes are reflected in report counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is at real git subprocess/OS interaction: unborn-HEAD repos return specific git errors/exit behavior. A fake adapter can miss true command/exit-code handling faults. REJECTION-GATE: behavior text contains no LLM/model dependency and no chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, execute the CLI subprocess, and assert it exits successfully and prints an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references "actual change history of the repository," so correctness must be validated against real git history/state transitions, not mocked data. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): yes, real subprocess/git behavior is intrinsic, so Integration is too low. REJECTION-GATE: no AI/ML or multi-agent sequencing in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Programmatically construct a real repo history containing added, deleted, renamed, and modified files across commits; run CLI and assert each category count equals expected totals from that history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This depends on true git index vs working-tree semantics and how the CLI invokes git for each mode. MOCK-HIDE: mocked status/diff outputs can hide real mismatches caused by command flags or subprocess wiring. REJECTION-GATE: behavior does not involve model judgment or multi-step agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent file changes once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI in both states, and assert structured stdout outputs are identical.
